mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.1% represents an emergency-level monetary accommodation. This ultra-low rate regime directly compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE at 8.4%) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This contraction in household income growth depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
regime_hint: labor market weakness and early-cycle contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1496k units) remain elevated, indicating sustained residential construction demand and consumer confidence in housing markets. This construction activity drives employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as near-term economic momentum is confirmed.
regime_hint: housing-led expansion with strong residential demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations when HOUST > 1400k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 101.36) shows manufacturing capacity utilization near trend. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, this signals supply-side constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × FEDFUNDS) shows zero or positive correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 535,982M) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS at 140,914k) jointly signal consumer spending capacity and labor income stability. When both series show month-over-month growth above their 3-month moving averages, this confirms demand momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both grow > 3-month MA simultaneously
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand and employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS + PAYEMS growth) shows negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 259.681) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.1%) creates a regime where real rates are deeply negative. This negative real rate environment erodes bond returns and forces institutional capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as portfolio reallocation accelerates.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation above target
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the real rate spread (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) shows zero or negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 140,914k) shows month-over-month growth. When PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 50k new jobs per month while UNRATE remains stable, this signals early-stage labor market softening that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50k AND UNRATE unchanged or rising
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the deceleration signal (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE change) shows positive correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 17,282.188B) growth rate combined with housing starts (HOUST at 1496k) jointly measure demand-side momentum. When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds 2.5% annualized and HOUST remains above 1400k units, this confirms robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% annualized AND HOUST > 1400k
regime_hint: strong demand-led expansion with housing-driven growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint demand signal (GDPC1 growth × HOUST level) shows negative or zero correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).
